meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THE PLAN UP TO DATE AS YOU EVOLVE THE CODE. DEVIATIONS TO THE PLAN MUST BE LOGGED WITHIN THE PLAN. THE PLAN MUST BE REASSESSED HOLISTICALLY AFTER ANY CHANGE OF ANY SIZE.
meta.completionPolicy: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE COMPLETED 100% OF THE BLUEPRINT. DO NOT STOP. YOU MUST REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS TO KEEP THE BLUEPRINT FULL. NEVER SPECIFY ANY ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST — ESPECIALLY NOT TIME ESTIMATES. NEVER SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND.
meta.blueprintRefinement: THIS BLUEPRINT MUST BE CONTINUALLY REFINED. After completing any task, before starting the next, re-read this file and assess whether it still accurately captures reality. If any task description is stale, wrong, unclear, or missing context — FIX IT NOW. The blueprint is a living document. A stale blueprint is worse than no blueprint. Refine aggressively: split tasks that are too coarse, merge tasks that are redundant, rewrite descriptions so a cold-start reader can act on them, and add tasks you discover along the way.

globalAlerts.warning: ON TOOLS: Use config.mk to create custom targets to run with Make. ALWAYS use custom targets that limit the amount of output you receive. For example, piping through tail, with FEW lines output. Prior to tail, pipe to tee. The file ./build.log in the root of the project is gitignored, so use that. That way you can search the output. Timing dependent tests are BANNED. As are those that take too long to run. Abide. OBEY.

CLI ENTRY POINT: The CLI wrapper wraps ollama launch claude, NOT just claude. The full invocation path involves: (1) ollama launch claude --config, (2) a TUI model selection menu that MUST be navigated programmatically over PTY, (3) then the Claude Code session itself. The model selection menu is a CRITICAL MANDATORY PATH.
NAMING: The word 'orchestrator' is RETIRED as a module/package name. The correct name is 'claude-mux' or similar (finalised by T031). If you see 'orchestrator' in code or docs, it MUST be corrected.
FEEDBACK CHANNEL: MCP is the PRIMARY means by which Claude Code instances send structured data back to OSM. PTY is for session lifecycle ONLY.
DECISIONS: Every task previously framed as a 'decision' or 'evaluation' reached the WRONG conclusion. The correct answer was always: follow the spec, use the proper library, do the actual work.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: Tasks are grouped to be executed in manageable units. Do not attempt to solve all tasks in a single turn.
mandatoryDirectives.subagentPolicy: Use subagents for execution of distinct task groups, and for the mandatory Rule of Two review gate. Serial only.
mandatoryDirectives.blueprintRefinement: Continual blueprint refinement is MANDATORY. After every task completion, re-read the blueprint and fix stale descriptions. Add discovered work. Split coarse tasks.

statusSection.currentState: CRITICAL REWORK PHASE. Four pre-existing test failures require immediate fixing (T001-T003). Core infrastructure migrations blocked on eventloop replacement (T011). Architecture doc rewrite (T031) gates claude-mux implementation. The 'orchestrator' package name is RETIRED. Goal autodiscovery test leak FIXED. sequentialTasks.task: T001: Fix data race in storage path globals with cleanup scheduler
sequentialTasks.description: DONE. Added sync.RWMutex (pathsMu) guarding function-variable overrides in paths.go. Created getSessionDirectory(), getSessionLockFilePath() accessor functions. Updated all production call sites in inspector.go, cleanup.go, paths.go, global_metadata_test.go. Verified: go test -race passes, make passes.

sequentialTasks.task: T002: Fix PTY test failures (WorkingDirectory + EchoHello)
sequentialTasks.description: DONE. Root cause: macOS PTY loses buffered data when slave fd closes before master reads. Fixed by keeping slave fd alive in parent process until process exits — slave is closed in the Wait() goroutine after the child exits but before EOF propagates. Also fixed EvalSymlinks for macOS /var -> /private/var resolution. Changed from creack/pty.StartWithSize to manual pty.Open() + cmd.Start() to retain slave fd control. All PTY tests pass 5x with -race.
DONE. Test passes 3x with -race when run in isolation and with full package. Original failure was load-dependent flake, not a code bug.

sequentialTasks.task: T004: Full make pass — all tests green
sequentialTasks.description: DONE. make make-all-with-log passes with zero failures. All linters (vet, staticcheck, betteralign, deadcode) pass. All tests pass with -race. Also fixed bubbletea TestRunProgram_Lifecycle race (added 50ms sleep before closing slave PTY fd to let handleResize goroutine finish).
Run 'go test -race -count=3 ./...' to surface intermittent races. Fix any found. Focus: storage globals, cleanup scheduler, eventloop lifecycle, session locking.

Unlocks: goja-grpc (T012), Promise-based fetch (T013), AbortSignal (T015). Imported in 14+ files. Rework internal/builtin/fetch/ for browser Fetch API compliance. fetch() returns Promise<Response>. Response/Headers/Request objects. Proper error types. Delete fetchStream() hack. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T169: Implement `osm mcp prompt` polymorphic terminal wrapper
sequentialTasks.description: Create `osm mcp` subcommand system with `osm mcp prompt` as polymorphic terminal wrapper. Style DIRECTLY after Anthropic's computer use interface for Claude - extensive research REQUIRED. Implementation must include: (1) Dynamic tool/command availability that changes based on context/mode - as user navigates between built-in commands (code-review, prompt-flow, goal, etc.), the available tools must morph. (2) Terminal UI that presents available tools, accepts commands, displays results. (3) Mode detection - infer current mode from context (git state, file changes, goal stage). (4) go-prompt integration with custom completers per mode. (5) Tool schema generation per mode. Research: Study Anthropic's computer use demo, their tool calling protocol, how they handle dynamic tool availability, their terminal presentation. This is NOT a simple CLI wrapper - it should feel like an intelligent assistant that knows what tools are relevant at each moment. Document research findings. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T170: Implement `osm mcp make` as port of mcp-server-make
sequentialTasks.description: Port github.com/joeycumines/mcp-server-make to osm as `osm mcp make` subcommand. Steps: (1) Research mcp-server-make - study its implementation, tool definitions, Makefile parsing, target discovery, execution model. (2) Analyze how it integrates with Claude Code / MCP clients. (3) Implement within osm codebase - integrate with existing command registry and MCP infrastructure. (4) Refactor/improve where beneficial: better error messages, improved target caching, cross-platform make detection, integration with osm's session system. (5) Ensure MCP protocol compliance - proper tool schema, notifications, state management. (6) Tests: target discovery, execution, error handling, cross-platform. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T171: Active agent steering via MCP enqueue + session-tied Unix socket communication (gRPC)
sequentialTasks.description: CRITICAL INFRASTRUCTURE. Implement active steering of Claude Code agents via `osm mcp make` tool preemption/interrupt/guard. ALLOW USERS TO ENQUEUE MESSAGES TO RUNNING AGENTS. Steps: (1) Session-tied communication mechanism: MCP process creates and listens on a Unix socket (session-bound), discovered via session directory. Windows fallback: TCP port. Communication MUST be gRPC. MANDATORY BASELINE FILES TO PORT: (a) https://github.com/joeycumines/MacosUseSDK/blob/main/make/buf.mk - gRPC code generation integration, (b) https://github.com/joeycumines/MacosUseSDK/blob/main/make/go.mk - Go build pattern (identical to root Makefile), (c) https://github.com/joeycumines/MacosUseSDK/blob/main/Makefile - different pattern, (d) https://github.com/joeycumines/MacosUseSDK/blob/main/buf.gen.yaml - gRPC generator config, (e) https://github.com/joeycumines/MacosUseSDK/blob/main/google-api-linter.yaml - mandatory linting rules, (f) https://github.com/joeycumines/MacosUseSDK/tree/main/hack - build helpers and scripts. (2) File-based session locking for synchronization - use existing osm session lock files to coordinate access and indicate pending messages. (3) Implement enqueue protocol: user invokes make tool with special target (e.g., `make steer MSG="..."`) that writes to session's communication channel, MCP server reads and forwards to Claude Code. (4) Preemption: when enqueue is detected, MCP server sends interrupt/guard signal to Claude Code, forcing it to respond to the enqueued message before continuing. (5) Tool schema: add `enqueue`, `interrupt`, `status` tools to mcp-make. (6) Integration with session lifecycle: cleanup sockets on session end. (7) Tests: enqueue during agent execution, preemption response, socket cleanup, cross-platform (Unix socket vs TCP). This is a LARGE task spanning MCP, session management, gRPC, IPC, and PTY control. Document architecture decisions. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T172: Implement `osm mcp parent` - Claude Code wrapper with stdio MCP + session socket
sequentialTasks.description: IMPLEMENT THE AGENTIC WORKFLOW. Create `osm mcp parent` (or pick short descriptive name like `osm mcp agent`) that WRAPS CLAUDE CODE as a child process. This is the CORE enabling infrastructure for the claude-mux agentic workflow. Steps: (1) Name selection: choose short descriptive name for the API surface - consider `osm mcp agent`, `osm mcp wrap`, `osm mcp claude`, or `osm mcp parent`. The name should clearly indicate it spawns/manages a child Claude Code instance. (2) Child process spawning: spawn Claude Code as child process with stdio MCP support enabled (`--mcp-stdio` or equivalent). (3) MCP stdio: child Claude Code communicates with parent MCP via stdio transport. (4) Session socket: child Claude Code ALSO connects to the session-tied Unix socket (from T171) for bidirectional communication with parent - this enables steering, enqueue, and control. (5) Bidirectional messaging: parent can send messages to child via session socket, child sends progress/results back. (6) Lifecycle management: proper startup sequencing, graceful shutdown, crash recovery, session cleanup. (7) Tool schema: expose tools for spawning, messaging, status, kill of child Claude instances. (8) Integration with osm session system: session-bound lifecycle, lock file coordination. (9) Tests: spawning, stdio communication, socket communication, shutdown, crash recovery. This is BLOCKING for the claude-mux agentic workflow - it is how Claude Code instances are wrapped and controlled. Run make.

sequentialTasks.task: T173: Implement macOS process hierarchy fallback for session ID detection
sequentialTasks.description: IMPLEMENT DEEP ANCHOR FOR MACOS. Currently macOS is the only OS without full process hierarchy fallback - per docs/reference/sophisticated-auto-determination-of-session-id.md it falls back to UUID when TERM_SESSION_ID is not set (e.g., Alacritty, Kitty, WezTerm, IDEs). Use cases: IDEs and tools that dont set the terminal ID env var. Steps: (1) Implement Darwin-specific Deep Anchor walk similar to Linux implementation. (2) Use sysctl or libproc APIs to walk process tree (macOS lacks /proc). (3) Identify stable anchor: skip ephemeral wrappers (sudo, su, setsid, osm, etc.), find shell boundaries (bash, zsh, fish), detect root boundaries (login, sshd). (4) Use process start time for PID recycling detection (via sysctl or libproc). (5) Integrate with existing session ID framework - use same namespace `anchor` and hash format. (6) Update docs/reference/sophisticated-auto-determination-of-session-id.md to mark macOS as Complete. (7) Tests: verify session persistence across terminal restarts in non-Terminal.app environments, verify IDE integration. Run make.
Add 3-5 additional built-in goals. stateVars, hotSnippets, flagDefs. Tests. Run make.
